forecasts.reasoning:
The question asks whether Donald Trump will visit China before July 1, 2025. Today's date is June 15, 2025.
On June 5-6, 2025, Trump and Xi Jinping had a phone call, after which Trump publicly stated he accepted an invitation to visit China and teams would meet "shortly" to determine a location and time. 
However, there are only 15 days left until July 1. State visits to China by a US President typically require massive logistical preparation, security cordons, protocol arrangements, etc., which usually take weeks or months to finalize. Thus, while a visit is firmly on the agenda, it's highly ambitious to coordinate and execute a presidential visit to Beijing within merely 2-3 weeks of the initial post-call announcement, especially when there's a scheduled G7 summit in Canada from June 15 to 17. 
Moreover, no dates have been announced yet. A late-June visit is possible, but given the timeframe, it's more likely the visit will occur in July, August, or the autumn. The G20 is usually later in the year. If they wanted to do it in June, it would probably already be officially scheduled and announced by mid-June, or rumors of an imminent trip next week would be in the news. Since there is no such news as of June 15, the probability of it happening before July 1 is fairly low. I estimate 25% just in case there is a rapid-fire summit arranged for late June.
